revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:庚]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{十干}} '''庚'''（こう、かのえ）は、[[十干]]の7番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[金]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「かのえ」（金の兄）ともいう。
庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。
「火剋金」と言って、金は火に伏せられるとされることから、火性の最も盛んな夏の時期の庚の日は凶であるとされる。そこで、夏の間の3回の庚の日を[[三伏]]という。
==庚を含む干支== *[[庚午]] *[[庚辰]] *[[庚寅]] *[[庚子]] *[[庚戌]] *[[庚申]]

庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。

庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。

[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘプタン]]、[[ヘプチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を7つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。
「火剋金」と言って、金は火に伏せられるとされることから、火性の最も盛んな夏の時期の庚の日は凶であるとされる。そこで、夏の間の3回の庚の日を[[三伏]]という。
==庚を含む干支== {{Wiktionary|庚}} *[[庚午]] *[[庚辰]] *[[庚寅]] *[[庚子]] *[[庚戌]] *[[庚申]] ==関連項目== *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}} [[Category:干支|かのえ]]

{{十干十二支}} '''庚'''（こう、かのえ）は、[[十干]]の7番目である。
[[陰陽五行説]]では[[金]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「かのえ」（金の兄）ともいう。 ==概要== 庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。
[[西暦]]や[[神武天皇即位紀元|皇紀]]では、[[10]]の倍数の年が庚の[[年]]となる。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘプタン]]、[[ヘプチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を7つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。
「火剋金」と言って、金は火に伏せられるとされることから、火性の最も盛んな夏の時期の庚の日は凶であるとされる。そこで、夏の間の3回の庚の日を[[三伏]]という。
==庚を含む干支== {{Wiktionary|庚}} *[[庚午]] *[[庚辰]] *[[庚寅]] *[[庚子]] *[[庚戌]] *[[庚申]] ==関連項目== *{{prefix}} *{{intitle}} [[Category:干支|かのえ]]

{{十干十二支}} '''庚'''（こう、かのえ）は、[[十干]]の7番目である。
[[陰陽五行説]]では[[金]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「かのえ」（金の兄）ともいう。
== 概要 == * 庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。 * [[西暦]]や[[神武天皇即位紀元|皇紀]]では、下一桁が0の年及び[[10]]の倍数の年が庚の[[年]]となる。 * [[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘプタン]]、[[ヘプチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を7つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。 * 「火剋金」と言って、金は火に伏せられるとされることから、火性の最も盛んな夏の時期の庚の日は凶であるとされる。そこで、夏の間の3回の庚の日を[[三伏]]という。 * 反対側は、[[乙]]（きのと）。 * 恵方は、西南西やや西（255度）。
== 庚を含む干支 == {{Wiktionary|庚}} * [[庚午]] * [[庚辰]] * [[庚寅]] * [[庚子]] * [[庚戌]] * [[庚申]]

{{十干十二支}} '''庚'''（こう、かのえ）は、[[十干]]の7番目である。
[[陰陽五行説]]では[[金]]性の陽に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「かのえ」（金の兄）ともいう。
== 概要 == * 庚の字は同音の「更」につながり、植物の生長が止まって新たな形に変化しようとする状態として、7番目に宛てられた。 * [[西暦]]や[[神武天皇即位紀元|皇紀]]では、下一桁が0の年及び[[10]]の倍数の年が庚の[[年]]となる。なお、年を表す時の別名は'''上章'''<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref>。 * [[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘプタン]]、[[ヘプチル基]]など、[[炭素]]を7つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。 * 「火剋金」と言って、金は火に伏せられるとされることから、火性の最も盛んな夏の時期の庚の日は凶であるとされる。そこで、夏の間の3回の庚の日を[[三伏]]という。 * 反対側は、[[乙]]（きのと）。 * 恵方は、西南西やや西（255度）。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh_min_nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh_min_nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh_yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}
{| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Metal Horse|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|zh|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|ko|경오|fr|Cheval de métal|de|Metall-Pferd|en|Metal Horse|lt=庚午 (Yang Metal Horse)|lt_ko=경오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Horse|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dragon|ja|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|zh|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|ko|경진|fr|Dragon de métal|de|Metall-Drache|en|Metal Dragon|lt=庚辰 (Yang Metal Dragon)|lt_ko=경진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Tiger|ja|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|zh|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|ko|경인|fr|Tigre de métal|de|Metall-Tiger|en|Metal Tiger|lt=庚寅 (Yang Metal Tiger)|lt_ko=경인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Rat|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|zh|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|ko|경자|fr|Rat de métal|de|Metall-Ratte|en|Metal Rat|lt=庚子 (Yang Metal Rat)|lt_ko=경자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Rat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dog|ja|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|zh|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|ko|경술|fr|Chien de métal|de|Metall-Hund|en|Metal Dog|lt=庚戌 (Yang Metal Dog)|lt_ko=경술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|Metal Monkey|lt=庚申 (Yang Metal Monkey)|lt_ko=경신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}
{| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Metal Horse|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|zh|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|ko|경오|fr|Cheval de métal|de|Metall-Pferd|en|Metal Horse|lt=庚午 (Yang Metal Horse)|lt_ko=경오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Horse|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dragon|ja|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|zh|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|ko|경진|fr|Dragon de métal|de|Metall-Drache|en|Metal Dragon|lt=庚辰 (Yang Metal Dragon)|lt_ko=경진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Tiger|ja|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|zh|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|ko|경인|fr|Tigre de métal|de|Metall-Tiger|en|Metal Tiger|lt=庚寅 (Yang Metal Tiger)|lt_ko=경인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Rat|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|zh|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|ko|경자|fr|Rat de métal|de|Metall-Ratte|en|Metal Rat|lt=庚子 (Yang Metal Rat)|lt_ko=경자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Rat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dog|ja|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|zh|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|ko|경술|fr|Chien de métal|de|Metall-Hund|en|Metal Dog|lt=庚戌 (Yang Metal Dog)|lt_ko=경술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|Metal Monkey|lt=庚申 (Yang Metal Monkey)|lt_ko=경신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}
{| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Metal Horse|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|zh|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|ko|경오|fr|Cheval de métal|de|Metall-Pferd|en|Metal Horse|lt=庚午 (Yang Metal Horse)|lt_ko=경오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Horse|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dragon|ja|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|zh|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|ko|경진|fr|Dragon de métal|de|Metall-Drache|en|Metal Dragon|lt=庚辰 (Yang Metal Dragon)|lt_ko=경진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Tiger|ja|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|zh|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|ko|경인|fr|Tigre de métal|de|Metall-Tiger|en|Metal Tiger|lt=庚寅 (Yang Metal Tiger)|lt_ko=경인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Rat|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|zh|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|ko|경자|fr|Rat de métal|de|Metall-Ratte|en|Metal Rat|lt=庚子 (Yang Metal Rat)|lt_ko=경자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Rat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dog|ja|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|zh|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|ko|경술|fr|Chien de métal|de|Metall-Hund|en|Metal Dog|lt=庚戌 (Yang Metal Dog)|lt_ko=경술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|Metal Monkey|lt=庚申 (Yang Metal Monkey)|lt_ko=경신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Gĕng (tiĕng-găng)]][[sn:commons:Category:庚 (Heavenly Stem)]][[fr:Geng]][[gan:庚]][[hak:Kâng (thiên-kôn)]][[ja:庚]][[ko:경 (천간)]][[vi:Canh (Thiên can)]][[za:Geng (diengan)]][[zh-min-nan:Keⁿ (Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

(Thian-kan)]][[zh-yue:庚 (天干)]][[zh:庚]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1078415]] Wikidata name: gēng [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] and the [[:en:Japanese imperial year|Japanese imperial year]], years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
