Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.

== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.

== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.

== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Hieroglyph|WD=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|WD=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}
{| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yang Metal Horse|ja|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|zh|庚午|1=Yang Metal Horse|ko|경오|fr|Cheval de métal|de|Metall-Pferd|en|Metal Horse|lt=庚午 (Yang Metal Horse)|lt_ko=경오|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Horse|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dragon|ja|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|zh|1=Yang Metal Dragon|2=ja|庚辰|1=Yang Metal Dragon|ko|경진|fr|Dragon de métal|de|Metall-Drache|en|Metal Dragon|lt=庚辰 (Yang Metal Dragon)|lt_ko=경진|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dragon|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Tiger|ja|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|zh|1=Yang Metal Tiger|2=ja|庚寅|ko|경인|fr|Tigre de métal|de|Metall-Tiger|en|Metal Tiger|lt=庚寅 (Yang Metal Tiger)|lt_ko=경인|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Tiger|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Rat|ja|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|zh|1=Yang Metal Rat|2=ja|庚子|1=Yang Metal Rat|ko|경자|fr|Rat de métal|de|Metall-Ratte|en|Metal Rat|lt=庚子 (Yang Metal Rat)|lt_ko=경자|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Rat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yang Metal Dog|ja|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|zh|1=Yang Metal Dog|2=ja|庚戌|ko|경술|fr|Chien de métal|de|Metall-Hund|en|Metal Dog|lt=庚戌 (Yang Metal Dog)|lt_ko=경술|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yang Metal Dog|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|ja|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|zh|庚申|1=Kōshin|lt=Yang Metal Monkey|2=ja|3=庚申|4=zh|5=庚申|6=ko|7=경신|lt_ko=경신|8=fr|9=Singe de métal|10=de|11=Metall-Affe|ko|경신|fr|Singe de métal|de|Metall-Affe|en|Metal Monkey|lt=庚申 (Yang Metal Monkey)|lt_ko=경신|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kanoe''' (庚) is the seventh of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|qid=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=a0dd58eba788cd8752271f402210c796f92886338bfa451c7e9617a2481982c8JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTgxMA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=6a86a8891f4ccd00fe88b30a871815de00ead0e0a317ccf953deefdcaf081104JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuamFwYW5kaWN0LmNvbS8lRTUlQkElOUE&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=eb1c0d5c80b2bc66a4ae7da7164530eca3868442d4f307baf5f7ee45446cd517JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkv5bqa&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=db4d67a8a527f829cefb4646cac1cba9f47089aa4f8690e005585a274afa17a9JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kanoe+(%e5%ba%9a)+dictionary&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjU2L2thbm9lLWtvdS0lRTUlQkElOUEtJUUzJTgxJThCJUUzJTgxJUFFJUUzJTgxJTg4LSVFMyU4MSU5MyVFMyU4MSU4Ng&ntb=1</ref>
In the {{ill|Yin-Yang and Five Elements|ja|陰陽五行説|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Five Elements|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 陰陽五行思想}} theory, it is associated with '''yang metal (金の陽)''', which is why in Japan it is also called '''"Kanoe" (金の兄, "Elder Metal")'''.
== Pronunciations: 庚 (Stem 7) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Gēng * Korean: Gyeong (경) * Vietnamese: Canh * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Kō (こう) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kanoe (かのえ, 金の兄 – “Elder Metal”)
== Overview ==
* The kanji '''庚''' is a {{ill|pictogram|ja|象形文字|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pictogram|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Hieroglyph|qid=Q193762}} representing a type of musical instrument.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng (季旭昇)| year=2014| title=説文新証| location=Taipei| publisher=芸文印書館| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=965|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al. (林志強等)| year=2017| title=《文源》評注| location=Beijing| publisher=中国社会科学出版社| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=97|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang (葛亮)| year=2022| title=漢字再発現――従旧識到新知| location=Shanghai| publisher=上海書画出版社| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| pages=106–9|qq=}}</ref> The use of this character to represent the seventh stem is due to {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10888485}}. * In the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|qid=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} and the {{ill|Japanese imperial year|en|Japanese imperial year|qid=Q1209728|lt=Japanese imperial year|1=Japanese imperial year}}, years ending in '''0''' (i.e., multiples of '''10''') correspond to '''Kō years (庚の年)'''. Another name for such years is '''Jōshō (上章)'''.<ref>{{Cite web |title=古代天干地支的别称及解释_书法欣赏_词典网 |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref>. * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|中国語の化合物命名法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, '''Kō (庚)''' is used for '''heptane (ヘプタン)''', '''heptyl groups (ヘプチル基)''', and other compounds containing '''seven carbon atoms'''. * According to the Five Elements' destructive cycle, '''"Fire conquers Metal" (火剋金)''', meaning metal is subdued by fire. Thus, in traditional beliefs, days of '''Kō during the peak fire season of summer''' are considered inauspicious. These three Kō days in summer are called '''Sanpuku (三伏)'''. * Its counterpart in the '''Ten Stems polarity''' is '''Otsu (乙)'''. * '''Eho (恵方, auspicious direction)''' for '''Kō years''' is '''west-southwest, slightly west (255°).'''
== Sexagenary cycles containing Kanoe (庚) == {{Wiktionary|庚}}
